name: gh-720-parallel-wave-contamination
description: Parallel implement wave shared one worktree and one state file: task 1's uncommitted edit broke a globally-sensitive spec, and task 4's RED probe captured task 1's test file as its own RED evidence (cross-task contamination). Viewed at task 4's boundary against task 4's OWN commits: nothing was ever done.

baseRun.notes: task 4's own test surface never existed; the recorded RED belonged to a sibling task's file

headRun.notes: no derived test files in task 4's diff — there is no diff

expected.rationale: Per-task verification over the task's own commits is structurally immune to sibling working-tree contamination: task 4 has an empty diff, a missing deliverable and zero derived tests on head (the recorded RED belonged to a sibling), so it is re-dispatched cleanly. Fail-on-base (I3) never contradicts by itself — the pass-on-base here is subsumed by I1/I4.

provenance.notes: Contaminated record: cycles[0].red.testFiles = ['factories/hookEntrypoint/__tests__/hookEntrypoint.test.js'] (task 1's file inside task 4's phase state). Scope attribution for parallel waves remains a Phase 5 item.
